question.english: Do singers play an important role in your country?
question.chinese: 在你所在的地方，歌手扮演重要角色嗎？

source_lines.text: Topic: Do singers play an important role in your country?
在你所在的地方，歌手扮演重要角色嗎？

components.english: Yes, definitely. In Hong Kong, I would say singers do play an important role, especially because music is such a strong part of popular culture and everyday emotion.
components.chinese: 是的，絕對有。在香港，我會說歌手的確扮演著重要角色，尤其因為音樂本來就是流行文化和日常情感中很重要的一部分。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: Yes, definitely. In Hong Kong, I would say singers do play an important
role, especially because music is such a strong part of popular culture and
everyday emotion.
[是的，絕對有。在香港，我會說歌手的確扮演著重要角色，尤其因為音樂本來就是流行文
化和日常情感中很重要的一部分。]

components.english: A good singer does more than just entertain people. Very often, they give voice to feelings that people struggle to express on their own, whether that is joy, heartbreak, nostalgia, or hope. In a fast-moving city like Hong Kong, songs can become little emotional landmarks in people’s lives. In that sense, singers are not just performers; they are part of the city’s emotional soundtrack.
components.chinese: 一位好的歌手不只是娛樂大眾而已。很多時候，他們其實是在替人說出那些人自己未必容易說出口的感受，不論是快樂、心碎、懷念，還是希望。在像香港這樣節奏很快的城市裡，歌曲常常會變成人生裡一個個小小的情感地標。從這個角度來看，歌手不只是表演者；他們也是這座城市情感配樂的一部分。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: A good singer does more than just entertain people. Very often,
they give voice to feelings that people struggle to express on their own, whether
that is joy, heartbreak, nostalgia, or hope. In a fast-moving city like Hong Kong,
songs can become little emotional landmarks in people’s lives. In that sense,
singers are not just performers; they are part of the city’s emotional soundtrack.
[一位好的歌手不只是娛樂大眾而已。很多時候，他們其實是在替人說出那些人自己未必容
易說出口的感受，不論是快樂、心碎、懷念，還是希望。在像香港這樣節奏很快的城市裡，歌
曲常常會變成人生裡一個個小小的情感地標。從這個角度來看，歌手不只是表演者；他們也
是這座城市情感配樂的一部分。]

components.english: For example, many people in Hong Kong grow up with songs linked to TV dramas, school days, karaoke memories, or certain phases of life. Years later, hearing just a few lines can take them straight back to a particular time, almost like a key turning in an old door.
components.chinese: 例如，很多香港人成長時都會有一些歌曲，和電視劇、學生時代、唱 K 回憶，或者人生某個階段連在一起。很多年後，只要再聽到其中幾句歌詞，就可能立刻把人帶回某個特定時刻，幾乎像一把鑰匙打開了一道舊門。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, many people in Hong Kong grow up with songs linked
to TV dramas, school days, karaoke memories, or certain phases of life. Years
later, hearing just a few lines can take them straight back to a particular time,
almost like a key turning in an old door.
[例如，很多香港人成長時都會有一些歌曲，和電視劇、學生時代、唱 K 回憶，或者人生某個
階段連在一起。很多年後，只要再聽到其中幾句歌詞，就可能立刻把人帶回某個特定時刻，
幾乎像一把鑰匙打開了一道舊門。]

components.english: So yes, singers are important, because they help shape not only entertainment, but also memory, feeling, and cultural identity.
components.chinese: 所以，是的，歌手是重要的，因為他們塑造的不只是娛樂本身，也包括回憶、情感，以及文化身份。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So yes, singers are important, because they help shape not only
entertainment, but also memory, feeling, and cultural identity.
[所以，是的，歌手是重要的，因為他們塑造的不只是娛樂本身，也包括回憶、情感，以及文
化身份。]

components.english: On top of that, singers often influence fashion, language, and public taste, although their role today is a little more spread out than it used to be.
components.chinese: 除此之外，歌手往往也會影響時尚、語言，以及大眾品味，只不過今天他們的影響力比起以前，分散了一點。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: On top of that, singers often influence fashion, language, and public
taste, although their role today is a little more spread out than it used to be.
[除此之外，歌手往往也會影響時尚、語言，以及大眾品味，只不過今天他們的影響力比起
以前，分散了一點。]

components.english: In the past, singers often stood closer to the centre of popular culture, because people had fewer channels competing for their attention. Now the spotlight is shared with influencers, short videos, and all kinds of online content. Even so, singers still matter. A hit song can ripple through the city very quickly: people talk about it, sing it, quote it, and build moods and moments around it. A strong singer does not just release music; they create an atmosphere.
components.chinese: 在過去，歌手通常更接近流行文化的中心，因為那時能爭奪人們注意力的渠道沒有現在那麼多。如今，鎂光燈已經要和網紅、短片，以及各式各樣的網上內容一起分享。不過，即使如此，歌手仍然很重要。一首大熱歌曲可以很快在整座城市擴散開來：人們會討論它、唱它、引用它，甚至圍繞它建立某種情緒和時刻。一位有影響力的歌手不只是推出音樂；他們其實是在創造一種氛圍。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: In the past, singers often stood closer to the centre of popular
culture, because people had fewer channels competing for their attention. Now
the spotlight is shared with influencers, short videos, and all kinds of online
content. Even so, singers still matter. A hit song can ripple through the city very
quickly: people talk about it, sing it, quote it, and build moods and moments
around it. A strong singer does not just release music; they create an
atmosphere.
[在過去，歌手通常更接近流行文化的中心，因為那時能爭奪人們注意力的渠道沒有現在那
麼多。如今，鎂光燈已經要和網紅、短片，以及各式各樣的網上內容一起分享。不過，即使如
此，歌手仍然很重要。一首大熱歌曲可以很快在整座城市擴散開來：人們會討論它、唱它、
引用它，甚至圍繞它建立某種情緒和時刻。一位有影響力的歌手不只是推出音樂；他們其實
是在創造一種氛圍。]

components.english: For instance, when a singer releases a song that really strikes a chord, people may use it in videos, sing it at gatherings, and keep hearing it in cafés, shops, and on public transport. Before long, it starts to feel as though the whole city is humming the same tune.
components.chinese: 例如，當一位歌手推出一首真正打動人心的歌時，人們可能會把它放進影片裡、在聚會時唱它，然後又在咖啡店、商店，甚至公共交通工具上不斷聽到它。過不了多久，你甚至會覺得整座城市都在哼著同一首旋律。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, when a singer releases a song that really strikes a
chord, people may use it in videos, sing it at gatherings, and keep hearing it in
cafés, shops, and on public transport. Before long, it starts to feel as though the
whole city is humming the same tune.
[例如，當一位歌手推出一首真正打動人心的歌時，人們可能會把它放進影片裡、在聚
會時唱它，然後又在咖啡店、商店，甚至公共交通工具上不斷聽到它。過不了多久，你
甚至會覺得整座城市都在哼著同一首旋律。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say singers do play an important role in Hong Kong, not only as entertainers but also as cultural figures who shape mood, memory, and identity. Their influence may not be as singular as it once was, but it is still real, still visible, and still very much alive.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說歌手在香港的確扮演著重要角色，他們不只是娛樂人物，也是塑造情緒、回憶，以及身份認同的文化人物。雖然他們今天的影響力也許不像從前那樣集中而單一，但它仍然是真實的、看得見的，而且依然非常鮮活。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say singers do play an important role in Hong
Kong, not only as entertainers but also as cultural figures who shape mood,
memory, and identity. Their influence may not be as singular as it once was, but
it is still real, still visible, and still very much alive.
[總結來說，我會說歌手在香港的確扮演著重要角色，他們不只是娛樂人物，也是塑造
情緒、回憶，以及身份認同的文化人物。雖然他們今天的影響力也許不像從前那樣集
中而單一，但它仍然是真實的、看得見的，而且依然非常鮮活。]

question.english: Do you think celebrities have a lot of income?
question.chinese: 主題：你認為名人收入很多嗎？

source_lines.text: Topic: Do you think celebrities have a lot of income?
[主題：你認為名人收入很多嗎？]

components.english: Yes, I think many celebrities do earn a lot of money, especially the ones who are well established or constantly in the public eye.
components.chinese: 是的，我認為很多名人的確賺很多錢，尤其是那些已經站穩地位，或者長期處於公眾視線中的人。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: Yes, I think many celebrities do earn a lot of money, especially the ones
who are well established or constantly in the public eye.
[是的，我認為很多名人的確賺很多錢，尤其是那些已經站穩地位，或者長期處於公眾視線
中的人。]

components.english: The main reason is that celebrities often do not rely on just one source of income. They may earn money from films, music, advertisements, appearances, brand deals, and social media all at once. Once someone becomes a big name, their popularity can start working for them around the clock. In that sense, fame can turn into a money-making machine.
components.chinese: 主要原因是，名人很多時候並不只依賴單一收入來源。他們可能同時從電影、音樂、廣告、公開活動、品牌合作，以及社交媒體中賺錢。當一個人成為有分量的名字之後，他的人氣往往就會開始全天候替他工作。從這個角度來說，名氣確實可以變成一部賺錢機器。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main reason is that celebrities often do not rely on just one
source of income. They may earn money from films, music, advertisements,
appearances, brand deals, and social media all at once. Once someone becomes a
big name, their popularity can start working for them around the clock. In that
sense, fame can turn into a money-making machine.
[主要原因是，名人很多時候並不只依賴單一收入來源。他們可能同時從電影、音樂、廣告、
公開活動、品牌合作，以及社交媒體中賺錢。當一個人成為有分量的名字之後，他的人氣往
往就會開始全天候替他工作。從這個角度來說，名氣確實可以變成一部賺錢機器。]

components.english: For example, a famous actor may be paid not only for acting in a film, but also for attending events, endorsing products, and appearing in campaigns. In the same way, a singer may earn from concerts, streaming, sponsorships, and merchandise. So the money can come in from several directions at once.
components.chinese: 例如，一位出名的演員不只會因拍電影而得到報酬，也可能會因出席活動、代言產品，以及參與宣傳企劃而賺錢。同樣地，一位歌手也可以從演唱會、串流收入、贊助，以及周邊產品中獲利。所以，金錢很多時候是從不同方向同時流進來的。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, a famous actor may be paid not only for acting in a
film, but also for attending events, endorsing products, and appearing in
campaigns. In the same way, a singer may earn from concerts, streaming,
sponsorships, and merchandise. So the money can come in from several
directions at once.
[例如，一位出名的演員不只會因拍電影而得到報酬，也可能會因出席活動、代言產品，以
及參與宣傳企劃而賺錢。同樣地，一位歌手也可以從演唱會、串流收入、贊助，以及周邊產
品中獲利。所以，金錢很多時候是從不同方向同時流進來的。]

components.english: So yes, in many cases, celebrities do have a lot of income, because fame often opens more than one door at the same time.
components.chinese: 所以，是的，在很多情況下，名人的確有很高收入，因為名氣往往會同時打開不只一道門。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So yes, in many cases, celebrities do have a lot of income, because
fame often opens more than one door at the same time.
[所以，是的，在很多情況下，名人的確有很高收入，因為名氣往往會同時打開不只一
道門。]

components.english: That said, I do not think all celebrities are necessarily rich, because fame and financial security are not always the same thing.
components.chinese: 不過，我也不認為所有名人都一定很有錢，因為出名和財務穩定並不總是同一回事。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I do not think all celebrities are necessarily rich, because
fame and financial security are not always the same thing.
[不過，我也不認為所有名人都一定很有錢，因為出名和財務穩定並不總是同一回

components.english: Some people may look glamorous on the surface, but their income can be uneven, short-lived, or heavily tied to trends. A person may be well known for a while and then quietly fade from the spotlight, and when the spotlight moves, the money can move with it. On top of that, some celebrities have high expenses, management costs, or unstable work, so things are not always as golden as they seem. In other words, fame may glitter, but it does not always guarantee a fortune.
components.chinese: 有些人表面看起來很風光，但他們的收入其實可能不穩定、維持不久，或者高度依賴潮流。一個人也許某段時間很有名，但之後就可能慢慢淡出焦點；而當聚光燈移開時，金錢有時也會跟著離開。除此之外，有些名人本身開支很高，也要支付經理人或團隊成本，而且工作機會並不穩定，所以情況未必總像表面那樣金光閃閃。換句話說，名氣會發亮，但未必一定等於大把財富。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: Some people may look glamorous on the surface, but their
income can be uneven, short-lived, or heavily tied to trends. A person may be
well known for a while and then quietly fade from the spotlight, and when the
spotlight moves, the money can move with it. On top of that, some celebrities
have high expenses, management costs, or unstable work, so things are not
always as golden as they seem. In other words, fame may glitter, but it does not
always guarantee a fortune.
[有些人表面看起來很風光，但他們的收入其實可能不穩定、維持不久，或者高度依賴
潮流。一個人也許某段時間很有名，但之後就可能慢慢淡出焦點；而當聚光燈移開時，
金錢有時也會跟著離開。除此之外，有些名人本身開支很高，也要支付經理人或團隊
成本，而且工作機會並不穩定，所以情況未必總像表面那樣金光閃閃。換句話說，名氣
會發亮，但未必一定等於大把財富。]

components.english: For instance, a minor actor, a reality-show figure, or an online influencer may be recognised by many people but still not earn as much as the public assumes. They may look famous on screen, yet in real life they may still be hustling to stay relevant and financially secure.
components.chinese: 例如，一位配角演員、真人騷人物，或者網絡紅人，也許很多人都認得他，但他的收入未必有大眾想像中那麼高。他在螢幕上可能看起來很有名，但在現實裡卻仍然可能要很努力地維持曝光和收入。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, a minor actor, a reality-show figure, or an online
influencer may be recognised by many people but still not earn as much as the
public assumes. They may look famous on screen, yet in real life they may still be
hustling to stay relevant and financially secure.
[例如，一位配角演員、真人騷人物，或者網絡紅人，也許很多人都認得他，但他的收
入未必有大眾想像中那麼高。他在螢幕上可能看起來很有名，但在現實裡卻仍然可能
要很努力地維持曝光和收入。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say many celebrities do earn a great deal, but not all of them are rolling in money. What people see is the spotlight; what they do not always see is how uncertain life can be behind it.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說很多名人的確賺很多錢，但並不是每一位都富得流油。人們看到的是聚光燈，而未必總看得到聚光燈背後那種不確定的生活。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion: Overall, I’d say many celebrities do earn a great deal, but not all
of them are rolling in money. What people see is the spotlight; what they do not
always see is how uncertain life can be behind it.
[總結來說，我會說很多名人的確賺很多錢，但並不是每一位都富得流油。人們看到的
是聚光燈，而未必總看得到聚光燈背後那種不確定的生活。]

question.english: Do you think CDs will have any role in the music industry in the future?
question.chinese: 你認為未來 CD 在音樂產業裡還會有角色嗎？

source_lines.text: Topic: Do you think CDs will have any role in the music industry in the future?
你認為未來 CD 在音樂產業裡還會有角色嗎？

components.english: Yes, I do think CDs will still have some role in the future, but it will probably be a much smaller one than before.
components.chinese: 是的，我的確認為未來 CD 仍然會有某種角色，只不過那個角色大概會比以前小得多。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: Yes, I do think CDs will still have some role in the future, but it
will probably be a much smaller one than before.
[是的，我的確認為未來 CD 仍然會有某種角色，只不過那個角色大概會比以前小得多。]

components.english: The main reason is that the way people listen to music has changed completely. Most people now want instant access, not a physical disc. They want to tap a screen, find a song in seconds, and listen anywhere, whether they are on the train, in the gym, or lying in bed. In that sense, streaming fits modern life like a glove, whereas CDs feel slower and less convenient. So, as a mainstream format, I think CDs have already had their moment in the sun.
components.chinese: 主要原因是，人們聽音樂的方式已經完全改變了。現在大多數人要的是即時接觸，而不是一張實體唱片。他們想按一下螢幕，幾秒內就找到歌曲，然後無論是在車上、健身室，還是躺在床上，都可以立刻播放。從這個角度來看，串流平台和現代生活簡直是天衣無縫，而 CD 則顯得較慢，也較不方便。所以，如果說的是主流格式，我認為 CD 的黃金時代其實已經過去了。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main reason is that the way people listen to music
has changed completely. Most people now want instant access, not a
physical disc. They want to tap a screen, find a song in seconds, and
listen anywhere, whether they are on the train, in the gym, or lying in
bed. In that sense, streaming fits modern life like a glove, whereas
CDs feel slower and less convenient. So, as a mainstream format, I
think CDs have already had their moment in the sun.
[主要原因是，人們聽音樂的方式已經完全改變了。現在大多數人要的是即時接觸，而不是一張實
體唱片。他們想按一下螢幕，幾秒內就找到歌曲，然後無論是在車上、健身室，還是躺在床上，都
可以立刻播放。從這個角度來看，串流平台和現代生活簡直是天衣無縫，而 CD 則顯得較慢，也
較不方便。所以，如果說的是主流格式，我認為 CD 的黃金時代其實已經過去了。]

components.english: For example, if someone hears a song they like today, they usually add it straight to a playlist. They do not normally go out, buy a disc, and play it on a separate machine. That old habit has faded quite a lot.
components.chinese: 例如，如果一個人今天聽到一首自己喜歡的歌，他通常會立刻把它加進歌單裡，而不會特地走出去買一張唱片，再用另一部機器播放。那種舊習慣其實已經淡了很多。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, if someone hears a song they like today,
they usually add it straight to a playlist. They do not normally go out,
buy a disc, and play it on a separate machine. That old habit has
faded quite a lot.
[例如，如果一個人今天聽到一首自己喜歡的歌，他通常會立刻把它加進歌單裡，而不會特地走出
去買一張唱片，再用另一部機器播放。那種舊習慣其實已經淡了很多。]

components.english: So, in everyday listening, I do not think CDs will remain very important, because convenience now calls the tune.
components.chinese: 所以，在日常聽歌這件事上，我不認為 CD 未來仍會很重要，因為現在真正主導節奏的，是方便性。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, in everyday listening, I do not think CDs will
remain very important, because convenience now calls the tune.
[所以，在日常聽歌這件事上，我不認為 CD 未來仍會很重要，因為現在真正主導節奏的，是方便

components.english: That said, I do not think CDs will disappear completely, because they still have emotional and collectible value.
components.chinese: 不過，我也不認為 CD 會完全消失，因為它仍然有情感價值和收藏價值。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I do not think CDs will disappear completely,
because they still have emotional and collectible value.
[不過，我也不認為 CD 會完全消失，因為它仍然有情感價值和收藏價值。]

components.english: A CD is not just a way of storing music; it is also a physical object that people can keep, display, or attach memories to. Some fans like the album artwork, the booklet, the lyrics, or the feeling of actually owning something. Others may buy CDs as gifts, souvenirs, or concert merchandise. In other words, CDs may stop being the engine of the industry, but they can still be part of its soul. They may no longer sit at the head of the table, but they will still have a seat in the room.
components.chinese: CD 不只是一種儲存音樂的方式；它同時也是一件可以被保存、展示，甚至被寄託回憶的實體物件。有些歌迷喜歡專輯封面、內頁、歌詞本，或者那種真正「擁有」某樣東西的感覺。另一些人則可能把 CD 當成禮物、紀念品，或者演唱會周邊來購買。換句話說，CD 也許不再是整個產業的引擎，但它仍然可能是那個產業靈魂的一部分。它未必還坐在主位上，但仍然會在房間裡保有一席之地。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: A CD is not just a way of storing music; it is also a
physical object that people can keep, display, or attach memories to.
Some fans like the album artwork, the booklet, the lyrics, or the
feeling of actually owning something. Others may buy CDs as gifts,
souvenirs, or concert merchandise. In other words, CDs may stop
being the engine of the industry, but they can still be part of its soul.
They may no longer sit at the head of the table, but they will still have
a seat in the room.
[CD 不只是一種儲存音樂的方式；它同時也是一件可以被保存、展示，甚至被寄託回憶的實體物
件。有些歌迷喜歡專輯封面、內頁、歌詞本，或者那種真正「擁有」某樣東西的感覺。另一些人則可
能把 CD 當成禮物、紀念品，或者演唱會周邊來購買。換句話說，CD 也許不再是整個產業的引擎
，但它仍然可能是那個產業靈魂的一部分。它未必還坐在主位上，但仍然會在房間裡保有一席之

components.english: For instance, a loyal fan may happily buy a special-edition CD from their favourite singer, not because it is the easiest way to hear the songs, but because it feels personal and worth keeping. In that case, the disc is almost like a keepsake rather than just a format.
components.chinese: 例如，一位忠實歌迷可能仍然會很開心地買下自己最喜歡歌手的特別版 CD，不是因為那是最方便的聽歌方式，而是因為它很有個人意義，也值得收藏。在這種情況下，那張唱片幾乎更像一件紀念品，而不只是一種格式。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, a loyal fan may happily buy a
special-edition CD from their favourite singer, not because it is the
easiest way to hear the songs, but because it feels personal and
worth keeping. In that case, the disc is almost like a keepsake rather
than just a format.
[例如，一位忠實歌迷可能仍然會很開心地買下自己最喜歡歌手的特別版 CD，不是因為那是最方
便的聽歌方式，而是因為它很有個人意義，也值得收藏。在這種情況下，那張唱片幾乎更像一件
紀念品，而不只是一種格式。]

components.english: Overall, I would say CDs will probably have a niche role in the future rather than a central one. They may no longer be the everyday heartbeat of the music industry, but they can still matter to collectors, devoted fans, and people who like music in a form they can actually hold.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說未來 CD 大概會扮演一個小眾角色，而不再是核心角色。它也許不再是音樂產業每天跳動的心臟，但對收藏者、忠實歌迷，以及那些喜歡把音樂真正拿在手上的人來說，它仍然是有分量的。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion: Overall, I would say CDs will probably have a niche
role in the future rather than a central one. They may no longer be the
everyday heartbeat of the music industry, but they can still matter to
collectors, devoted fans, and people who like music in a form they
can actually hold.
[總結來說，我會說未來 CD 大概會扮演一個小眾角色，而不再是核心角色。它也許不再是音樂產
業每天跳動的心臟，但對收藏者、忠實歌迷，以及那些喜歡把音樂真正拿在手上的人來說，它仍
然是有分量的。]

question.english: In your country, do people prefer to listen to traditional music or foreign music?
question.chinese: 在你所在的地方，人們比較喜歡聽傳統音樂，還是外國音樂？（以香港為例）

source_lines.text: Topic: In your country, do people prefer to listen to traditional music or
foreign music?
在你所在的地方，人們比較喜歡聽傳統音樂，還是外國音樂？（以香港為例）

components.english: If I had to choose between those two, I’d say people in Hong Kong probably listen to foreign music more often in everyday life.
components.chinese: 如果一定要在這兩者之間選，我會說香港人在日常生活裡大概還是更常聽外國音樂。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: If I had to choose between those two, I’d say people in Hong
Kong probably listen to foreign music more often in everyday life.
[如果一定要在這兩者之間選，我會說香港人在日常生活裡大概還是更常聽外國音樂。]

components.english: The main reason is that everyday listening now feels very international. If you look at current Hong Kong streaming charts, you can already see that people’s taste is pulled in many directions at once. Apple Music’s Hong Kong Top 100, for example, features a mix of local artists and non-local acts such as BTS and Jay Chou, which tells me that daily listening is much more pop-driven and outward-looking than traditional.
components.chinese: 主要原因是，現在日常聽歌這件事本身就很國際化。如果你看看香港現在的串流排行榜，就會發現大家的口味其實同時被很多不同方向吸引。以 Apple Music 的香港 Top 100 為例，裡面同時有本地歌手，也有像 BTS 和周杰倫這些非本地藝人，這會令我覺得，大家日常聽的音樂明顯更偏向流行、也更向外，而不是傳統類型。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main reason is that everyday listening now feels
very international. If you look at current Hong Kong streaming charts,
you can already see that people’s taste is pulled in many directions at
once. Apple Music’s Hong Kong Top 100, for example, features a mix
of local artists and non-local acts such as BTS and Jay Chou, which
tells me that daily listening is much more pop-driven and
outward-looking than traditional.
[主要原因是，現在日常聽歌這件事本身就很國際化。如果你看看香港現在的串流排行榜，
就會發現大家的口味其實同時被很多不同方向吸引。以 Apple Music 的香港 Top 100 為例
，裡面同時有本地歌手，也有像 BTS 和周杰倫這些非本地藝人，這會令我覺得，大家日常
聽的音樂明顯更偏向流行、也更向外，而不是傳統類型。]

components.english: For example, on an ordinary day, people are probably much more likely to have K-pop, Mandopop, English pop, or modern Cantopop in their earphones than Cantonese opera or traditional instrumental music. Traditional music is still there, of course, but it is usually not what most people reach for first when they just want something to listen to.
components.chinese: 例如，在很普通的一天裡，人們耳機裡更大機會出現的，通常會是 K-pop、華語流行、英文流行，或者現代廣東流行歌，而不是粵劇或傳統器樂。傳統音樂當然仍然存在，但當大多數人只是想隨手聽點甚麼時，它通常不是第一個被選中的。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, on an ordinary day, people are probably
much more likely to have K-pop, Mandopop, English pop, or modern
Cantopop in their earphones than Cantonese opera or traditional
instrumental music. Traditional music is still there, of course, but it is
usually not what most people reach for first when they just want
something to listen to.
[例如，在很普通的一天裡，人們耳機裡更大機會出現的，通常會是 K-pop、華語流行、英文
流行，或者現代廣東流行歌，而不是粵劇或傳統器樂。傳統音樂當然仍然存在，但當大多數
人只是想隨手聽點甚麼時，它通常不是第一個被選中的。]

components.english: So, in terms of casual, day-to-day listening, I’d say foreign music probably comes out ahead.
components.chinese: 所以，如果說的是輕鬆、日常的聽歌習慣，我會說外國音樂大概是比較佔上風的。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, in terms of casual, day-to-day listening, I’d say
foreign music probably comes out ahead.
[所以，如果說的是輕鬆、日常的聽歌習慣，我會說外國音樂大概是比較佔上風的。]

components.english: That said, I do not think traditional music is unimportant at all, because it still carries real cultural weight in Hong Kong.
components.chinese: 不過，我完全不覺得傳統音樂不重要，因為它在香港仍然有很真實、很有分量的文化地位。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I do not think traditional music is unimportant at
all, because it still carries real cultural weight in Hong Kong.
[不過，我完全不覺得傳統音樂不重要，因為它在香港仍然有很真實、很有分量的文化地

components.english: In Hong Kong, traditional forms such as Cantonese opera are treated as cultural heritage rather than just background entertainment. Official heritage sources describe Cantonese opera as a traditional theatre form deeply rooted in Cantonese-speaking regions, including Hong Kong, and recent reporting has also shown efforts to connect it with younger audiences through cross-genre performance. So even if it is not the soundtrack of daily life, it is still part of the city’s cultural heartbeat.
components.chinese: 在香港，像粵劇這類傳統形式，很多時候更像是文化遺產，而不只是一般背景娛樂。官方非遺資料把粵劇描述為深深扎根於粵語地區、包括香港在內的傳統戲曲形式，而近年的報道也顯示，有人正透過跨界演出嘗試把它帶給更年輕的觀眾。所以，即使它未必是大家每天生活裡的背景音樂，它仍然是這座城市文化脈搏的一部分。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation: In Hong Kong, traditional forms such as Cantonese
opera are treated as cultural heritage rather than just background
entertainment. Official heritage sources describe Cantonese opera as
a traditional theatre form deeply rooted in Cantonese-speaking
regions, including Hong Kong, and recent reporting has also shown
efforts to connect it with younger audiences through cross-genre
performance. So even if it is not the soundtrack of daily life, it is still
part of the city’s cultural heartbeat.
[在香港，像粵劇這類傳統形式，很多時候更像是文化遺產，而不只是一般背景娛樂。官方
非遺資料把粵劇描述為深深扎根於粵語地區、包括香港在內的傳統戲曲形式，而近年的報
道也顯示，有人正透過跨界演出嘗試把它帶給更年輕的觀眾。所以，即使它未必是大家每天
生活裡的背景音樂，它仍然是這座城市文化脈搏的一部分。]

components.english: For instance, people may not listen to Cantonese opera on the train every morning, but they still come across it in performances, cultural events, and family or community settings. In that sense, traditional music may not fill people’s playlists, but it still helps keep their roots alive.
components.chinese: 例如，人們未必會每天早上坐車時就播粵劇，但他們仍然會在表演、文化活動，以及家庭或社區場合裡接觸到它。從這個角度來說，傳統音樂也許未必塞滿大家的播放清單，但它仍然在幫助人們把自己的文化根保留下來。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, people may not listen to Cantonese opera
on the train every morning, but they still come across it in
performances, cultural events, and family or community settings. In
that sense, traditional music may not fill people’s playlists, but it still
helps keep their roots alive.
[例如，人們未必會每天早上坐車時就播粵劇，但他們仍然會在表演、文化活動，以及家庭
或社區場合裡接觸到它。從這個角度來說，傳統音樂也許未必塞滿大家的播放清單，但它仍
然在幫助人們把自己的文化根保留下來。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say people in Hong Kong probably prefer foreign music for everyday listening, but traditional music still holds a special place. One is what people play for pleasure; the other is what helps a culture remember itself.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說香港人在日常聽歌上大概比較偏向外國音樂，但傳統音樂仍然佔有一個很特別的位置。前者是人們拿來放鬆和享受的，後者則像是在幫一種文化記住自己。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say people in Hong Kong probably prefer
foreign music for everyday listening, but traditional music still holds a
special place. One is what people play for pleasure; the other is what
helps a culture remember itself.
[總結來說，我會說香港人在日常聽歌上大概比較偏向外國音樂，但傳統音樂仍然佔有一個
很特別的位置。前者是人們拿來放鬆和享受的，後者則像是在幫一種文化記住自己。]

question.english: Do you prefer live performances?
question.chinese: 你比較喜歡現場表演嗎？

source_lines.text: Topic: Do you prefer live performances?
你比較喜歡現場表演嗎？

components.english: Yes, I usually do, because live performances feel more immediate, more human, and much more emotionally alive.
components.chinese: 是的，我通常比較喜歡，因為現場表演會讓人覺得更即時、更有人味，也更有情感上的生命力。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: Yes, I usually do, because live performances feel more
immediate, more human, and much more emotionally alive.
[是的，我通常比較喜歡，因為現場表演會讓人覺得更即時、更有人味，也更有情感上的生
命力。]

components.english: The main reason is that there is something electric about being in the same space as the performers. You are not just watching a polished product on a screen; you are sharing a moment that is unfolding in real time. The voice, the movement, the atmosphere, even the audience’s reaction all become part of the experience. In that sense, a live performance feels less like content and more like an event.
components.chinese: 主要原因是，當你和表演者身處同一個空間時，那種感覺往往帶著一種很強的現場張力。你不只是在看螢幕上一個打磨好的成品；你其實是在參與一個正在眼前即時發生的時刻。聲音、動作、現場氣氛，甚至觀眾的反應，都會成為整個體驗的一部分。從這個角度來看，現場表演不像單純的內容，更像是一件真正發生的事情。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main reason is that there is something electric
about being in the same space as the performers. You are not just
watching a polished product on a screen; you are sharing a moment
that is unfolding in real time. The voice, the movement, the
atmosphere, even the audience’s reaction all become part of the
experience. In that sense, a live performance feels less like content
and more like an event.
[主要原因是，當你和表演者身處同一個空間時，那種感覺往往帶著一種很強的現場張力。
你不只是在看螢幕上一個打磨好的成品；你其實是在參與一個正在眼前即時發生的時刻。
聲音、動作、現場氣氛，甚至觀眾的反應，都會成為整個體驗的一部分。從這個角度來看，現
場表演不像單純的內容，更像是一件真正發生的事情。]

components.english: For example, if I go to a concert, a play, or even a live music performance in a small venue, I often feel much more drawn in than when I watch something online. Even a tiny imperfection can make it feel more real, as if the performance has a heartbeat of its own.
components.chinese: 例如，如果我去看演唱會、舞台劇，或者小型場地的現場音樂表演，我通常都會比在網上觀看時更投入。即使只是一些很小的瑕疵，也反而會令整個表演顯得更真實，彷彿它本身有自己的心跳一樣。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, if I go to a concert, a play, or even a live
music performance in a small venue, I often feel much more drawn in
than when I watch something online. Even a tiny imperfection can
make it feel more real, as if the performance has a heartbeat of its
own.
[例如，如果我去看演唱會、舞台劇，或者小型場地的現場音樂表演，我通常都會比在網上
觀看時更投入。即使只是一些很小的瑕疵，也反而會令整個表演顯得更真實，彷彿它本身有
自己的心跳一樣。]

components.english: So yes, I would say I prefer live performances, mainly because they feel richer, warmer, and more unforgettable.
components.chinese: 所以，是的，我會說我比較喜歡現場表演，主要因為它們往往顯得更豐富、更有溫度，也更令人難忘。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So yes, I would say I prefer live performances, mainly
because they feel richer, warmer, and more unforgettable.
[所以，是的，我會說我比較喜歡現場表演，主要因為它們往往顯得更豐富、更有溫度，也更
令人難忘。]

components.english: That said, I would not say live performances are always better in every situation, because recorded performances have their own advantages too.
components.chinese: 不過，我也不會說現場表演在所有情況下都一定更好，因為錄製好的表演同樣有它自己的優點。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I would not say live performances are always
better in every situation, because recorded performances have their
own advantages too.
[不過，我也不會說現場表演在所有情況下都一定更好，因為錄製好的表演同樣有它自己的
優點。]

components.english: To be honest, recorded performances are more convenient, more affordable, and easier to revisit. You can pause them, replay them, and enjoy them without worrying about ticket prices, travel time, or bad seats. Sometimes that practicality matters. In other words, live performances may have the magic, but recorded ones often win on comfort.
components.chinese: 老實說，錄製好的表演在方便程度、價格，以及重溫的可能性上都更有優勢。你可以暫停、重播，也不需要擔心票價、交通時間，或者座位不理想。有時這種實際性確實很重要。換句話說，現場表演也許擁有那份魔力，但錄製版本很多時候在舒適和方便上更勝一籌。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: To be honest, recorded performances are more
convenient, more affordable, and easier to revisit. You can pause
them, replay them, and enjoy them without worrying about ticket
prices, travel time, or bad seats. Sometimes that practicality matters.
In other words, live performances may have the magic, but recorded
ones often win on comfort.
[老實說，錄製好的表演在方便程度、價格，以及重溫的可能性上都更有優勢。你可以暫停、
重播，也不需要擔心票價、交通時間，或者座位不理想。有時這種實際性確實很重要。換句
話說，現場表演也許擁有那份魔力，但錄製版本很多時候在舒適和方便上更勝一籌。]

components.english: For instance, if I am tired after work, I might prefer watching a filmed concert or a performance at home rather than going all the way out. That way, I can still enjoy the art without turning the whole evening into a mission.
components.chinese: 例如，如果我下班後已經很累，我可能就會更想留在家裡看一場錄製好的演唱會或表演，而不是特地走很遠出去。那樣一來，我仍然可以享受藝術本身，而不用把整個晚上都搞成一場任務。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, if I am tired after work, I might prefer
watching a filmed concert or a performance at home rather than going
all the way out. That way, I can still enjoy the art without turning the
whole evening into a mission.
[例如，如果我下班後已經很累，我可能就會更想留在家裡看一場錄製好的演唱會或表演，
而不是特地走很遠出去。那樣一來，我仍然可以享受藝術本身，而不用把整個晚上都搞成一
場任務。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say I do prefer live performances when I have the chance, because they offer something a screen rarely can: real presence, shared energy, and a moment that exists only once.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說如果有機會的話，我的確更喜歡現場表演，因為它們能帶來一些螢幕很少真正能給到的東西：真實的在場感、共享的能量，以及一個只會出現一次的時刻。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say I do prefer live performances when I
have the chance, because they offer something a screen rarely can:
real presence, shared energy, and a moment that exists only once.
[總結來說，我會說如果有機會的話，我的確更喜歡現場表演，因為它們能帶來一些螢幕很
少真正能給到的東西：真實的在場感、共享的能量，以及一個只會出現一次的時刻。]

question.english: What do you think about the role of singers at the national level?
question.chinese: 主題：你怎樣看歌手在國家層面上的角色？

source_lines.text: What do you think about the role of singers at the national level?
主題：你怎樣看歌手在國家層面上的角色？

components.english: I think singers can play a surprisingly important role at the national level, because they are not just entertainers; they can also become cultural representatives.
components.chinese: 我認為歌手在國家層面上其實可以扮演相當重要的角色，因為他們不只是娛樂人物，同時也可能成為文化代表。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: I think singers can play a surprisingly important role at the
national level, because they are not just entertainers; they can also
become cultural representatives.
[我認為歌手在國家層面上其實可以扮演相當重要的角色，因為他們不只是娛樂人物，同時
也可能成為文化代表。]

components.english: When a singer becomes widely known, their music often carries the language, mood, stories, and style of a place. Through that, they can help shape cultural identity, strengthen a sense of belonging, and put their country or city on the map. In that sense, a singer can become a kind of cultural voice: not the whole nation, of course, but one voice that helps the nation be heard.
components.chinese: 當一位歌手變得廣為人知時，他們的音樂往往會承載一個地方的語言、情感、故事，以及風格。透過這些元素，他們能幫助塑造文化身份、加強歸屬感，甚至讓自己的國家或城市被更多人看見。從這個角度來說，歌手可以成為某種文化聲音：當然不是整個國家本身，但卻是幫助這個國家被聽見的一把聲音。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: When a singer becomes widely known, their music
often carries the language, mood, stories, and style of a place.
Through that, they can help shape cultural identity, strengthen a
sense of belonging, and put their country or city on the map. In that
sense, a singer can become a kind of cultural voice: not the whole
nation, of course, but one voice that helps the nation be heard.
[當一位歌手變得廣為人知時，他們的音樂往往會承載一個地方的語言、情感、故事，以及風
格。透過這些元素，他們能幫助塑造文化身份、加強歸屬感，甚至讓自己的國家或城市被更
多人看見。從這個角度來說，歌手可以成為某種文化聲音：當然不是整個國家本身，但卻是
幫助這個國家被聽見的一把聲音。]

components.english: For example, when a singer performs at a major national event, or when a local song becomes popular overseas, people do not only hear a melody; they also get a glimpse of the culture behind it. Sometimes one song can carry pride, memory, and identity further than a speech ever could.
components.chinese: 例如，當一位歌手在大型國家活動中表演，或者當一首本地歌曲在海外流行起來時，人們聽到的其實不只是旋律，還會同時看見其背後的文化。有時候，一首歌所承載的自豪感、回憶，以及身份認同，甚至比一篇演說走得更遠。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, when a singer performs at a major national
event, or when a local song becomes popular overseas, people do not
only hear a melody; they also get a glimpse of the culture behind it.
Sometimes one song can carry pride, memory, and identity further
than a speech ever could.
[例如，當一位歌手在大型國家活動中表演，或者當一首本地歌曲在海外流行起來時，人們
聽到的其實不只是旋律，還會同時看見其背後的文化。有時候，一首歌所承載的自豪感、回
憶，以及身份認同，甚至比一篇演說走得更遠。]

components.english: So, in my view, singers matter at the national level because they can carry a country’s cultural heartbeat far beyond the stage.
components.chinese: 所以，在我看來，歌手之所以在國家層面上重要，是因為他們能把一個地方的文化脈搏，帶到舞台以外更遠的地方。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, in my view, singers matter at the national level
because they can carry a country’s cultural heartbeat far beyond the
stage.
[所以，在我看來，歌手之所以在國家層面上重要，是因為他們能把一個地方的文化脈搏，帶
到舞台以外更遠的地方。]

components.english: That said, I do not think singers should be treated as perfect symbols or spokespersons for an entire nation.
components.chinese: 不過，我也不認為歌手應該被當成一個國家完美無缺的象徵，或者所有人的代言人。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I do not think singers should be treated as perfect
symbols or spokespersons for an entire nation.
[不過，我也不認為歌手應該被當成一個國家完美無缺的象徵，或者所有人的代言人。]

components.english: To be honest, they are artists first, not saints or politicians. They can inspire people, comfort people, and sometimes bring people together, but it would be unfair to place the weight of a whole nation on their shoulders. A nation is a choir, not a solo. A singer may lead the melody, but they cannot represent every voice, every background, or every opinion.
components.chinese: 老實說，他們首先是藝術工作者，而不是聖人，也不是政治人物。他們可以啟發人、安慰人，有時也能把人凝聚起來，但如果要他們扛起整個國家的重量，其實並不公平。一個國家像一個合唱團，而不是一場獨唱。歌手也許可以帶領旋律，但他不可能真正代表每一把聲音、每一種背景，或者每一種立場。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: To be honest, they are artists first, not saints or
politicians. They can inspire people, comfort people, and sometimes
bring people together, but it would be unfair to place the weight of a
whole nation on their shoulders. A nation is a choir, not a solo. A
singer may lead the melody, but they cannot represent every voice,
every background, or every opinion.
[老實說，他們首先是藝術工作者，而不是聖人，也不是政治人物。他們可以啟發人、安慰人
，有時也能把人凝聚起來，但如果要他們扛起整個國家的重量，其實並不公平。一個國家像
一個合唱團，而不是一場獨唱。歌手也許可以帶領旋律，但他不可能真正代表每一把聲音、
每一種背景，或者每一種立場。]

components.english: For instance, after a difficult social moment or during a major celebration, a singer may release a song that resonates deeply with the public and gives people something to hold on to emotionally. However, that does not mean everyone thinks the same way or sees that singer in exactly the same light.
components.chinese: 例如，在一個艱難的社會時刻之後，或者在一個重大的慶祝場合中，一位歌手也許會推出一首很能打動大眾的歌曲，讓人們在情感上有一樣可以抓住的東西。不過，這並不代表每個人都會用同一種方式去理解那首歌，或者用完全一樣的眼光去看待那位歌手。

source_lines.text: 7. Example 2: For instance, after a difficult social moment or during a
major celebration, a singer may release a song that resonates deeply
with the public and gives people something to hold on to emotionally.
However, that does not mean everyone thinks the same way or sees
that singer in exactly the same light.
[例如，在一個艱難的社會時刻之後，或者在一個重大的慶祝場合中，一位歌手也許會推出
一首很能打動大眾的歌曲，讓人們在情感上有一樣可以抓住的東西。不過，這並不代表每個
人都會用同一種方式去理解那首歌，或者用完全一樣的眼光去看待那位歌手。]

components.english: Overall, I think singers do have an important role at the national level: they can express culture, strengthen collective feeling, and project a place’s image to the outside world. But their role is meaningful rather than absolute. They can give a nation a soundtrack, but they are not the nation by themselves.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我認為歌手在國家層面上確實有重要角色：他們可以表達文化、加強集體情感，並且把一個地方的形象傳遞到外面。不過，他們的角色是有意義的，卻不是絕對的。他們可以為一個國家寫下配樂，但他們本身並不等於整個國家。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I think singers do have an important role at
the national level: they can express culture, strengthen collective
feeling, and project a place’s image to the outside world. But their role
is meaningful rather than absolute. They can give a nation a
soundtrack, but they are not the nation by themselves.
[總結來說，我認為歌手在國家層面上確實有重要角色：他們可以表達文化、加強集體情感，
並且把一個地方的形象傳遞到外面。不過，他們的角色是有意義的，卻不是絕對的。他們可
以為一個國家寫下配樂，但他們本身並不等於整個國家。]

question.english: Why do you think pop music is so popular?
question.chinese: 你為甚麼認為流行音樂這麼受歡迎？

source_lines.text: Topic: Why do you think pop music is so popular?
你為甚麼認為流行音樂這麼受歡迎？

components.english: I think pop music is so popular mainly because it is easy to enjoy and easy to remember.
components.chinese: 我認為流行音樂之所以這麼受歡迎，主要是因為它很容易欣賞，也很容易記住。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: I think pop music is so popular mainly because it is easy to
enjoy and easy to remember.
[我認為流行音樂之所以這麼受歡迎，主要是因為它很容易欣賞，也很容易記住。]

components.english: Pop songs are usually built to catch people quickly. They often have catchy hooks, simple structures, strong beats, and lyrics that are easy to follow, so even after hearing a song once or twice, people can already hum along. In that sense, pop music does not usually ask the listener to work too hard; it opens the door wide and invites them straight in. That kind of instant appeal is hard to beat.
components.chinese: 流行歌曲通常就是為了迅速吸引人而設計的。它們往往有上口的旋律、簡單的結構、鮮明的節拍，以及容易理解的歌詞，所以即使只聽一兩次，人們很多時候已經可以跟著哼唱。從這個角度來看，流行音樂通常不會要求聽眾花很大力氣去理解；它更像是把門大大打開，直接把人迎進去。這種即時吸引力確實很難被取代。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: Pop songs are usually built to catch people quickly.
They often have catchy hooks, simple structures, strong beats, and
lyrics that are easy to follow, so even after hearing a song once or
twice, people can already hum along. In that sense, pop music does
not usually ask the listener to work too hard; it opens the door wide
and invites them straight in. That kind of instant appeal is hard to
beat.
[流行歌曲通常就是為了迅速吸引人而設計的。它們往往有上口的旋律、簡單的結構、鮮明
的節拍，以及容易理解的歌詞，所以即使只聽一兩次，人們很多時候已經可以跟著哼唱。從
這個角度來看，流行音樂通常不會要求聽眾花很大力氣去理解；它更像是把門大大打開，直
接把人迎進去。這種即時吸引力確實很難被取代。]

components.english: For example, a person may hear a pop song in a café, a shopping centre, or on social media, and by the end of the day the chorus is still playing in their head. That is one reason pop spreads like wildfire: it sticks.
components.chinese: 例如，一個人可能只是在咖啡店、商場，或者社交媒體上聽到一首流行歌，但到那天結束時，副歌仍然在腦海裡繞來繞去。這正是流行音樂能迅速傳開的其中一個原因：它很黏耳。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, a person may hear a pop song in a café, a
shopping centre, or on social media, and by the end of the day the
chorus is still playing in their head. That is one reason pop spreads
like wildfire: it sticks.
[例如，一個人可能只是在咖啡店、商場，或者社交媒體上聽到一首流行歌，但到那天結束
時，副歌仍然在腦海裡繞來繞去。這正是流行音樂能迅速傳開的其中一個原因：它很黏耳。]

components.english: So, one big reason pop music is popular is that it is accessible, memorable, and immediately enjoyable.
components.chinese: 所以，流行音樂受歡迎其中一個很大的原因，就是它容易接近、容易記住，而且能立刻帶來享受。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, one big reason pop music is popular is that it is
accessible, memorable, and immediately enjoyable.
[所以，流行音樂受歡迎其中一個很大的原因，就是它容易接近、容易記住，而且能立刻帶
來享受。]

components.english: Another reason is that pop music is deeply woven into modern life and heavily supported by the media.
components.chinese: 另一個原因是，流行音樂早已深深融入現代生活，而且一直得到媒體的大力推動。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: Another reason is that pop music is deeply woven into
modern life and heavily supported by the media.
[另一個原因是，流行音樂早已深深融入現代生活，而且一直得到媒體的大力推動。]

components.english: Pop music is everywhere. It plays in shops, films, adverts, short videos, concerts, and streaming playlists, so people come into contact with it almost without trying. On top of that, pop stars are often marketed very strongly, which makes their music feel even more visible and socially relevant. In other words, pop music does not just live in people’s headphones; it lives in the air. It becomes part of conversations, trends, moods, and memories.
components.chinese: 流行音樂幾乎無處不在。它會出現在商店、電影、廣告、短片、演唱會，以及串流歌單裡，所以人們很多時候根本不用刻意去找，就已經和它接觸上了。除此之外，流行歌手通常也會被大力包裝和宣傳，令他們的音樂顯得更有存在感，也更貼近社會潮流。換句話說，流行音樂不只住在人的耳機裡；它很多時候像是住在空氣裡。它會慢慢成為談話、潮流、情緒，甚至回憶的一部分。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation: Pop music is everywhere. It plays in shops, films,
adverts, short videos, concerts, and streaming playlists, so people
come into contact with it almost without trying. On top of that, pop
stars are often marketed very strongly, which makes their music feel
even more visible and socially relevant. In other words, pop music
does not just live in people’s headphones; it lives in the air. It
becomes part of conversations, trends, moods, and memories.
[流行音樂幾乎無處不在。它會出現在商店、電影、廣告、短片、演唱會，以及串流歌單裡，
所以人們很多時候根本不用刻意去找，就已經和它接觸上了。除此之外，流行歌手通常也會
被大力包裝和宣傳，令他們的音樂顯得更有存在感，也更貼近社會潮流。換句話說，流行音
樂不只住在人的耳機裡；它很多時候像是住在空氣裡。它會慢慢成為談話、潮流、情緒，甚
至回憶的一部分。]

components.english: For instance, when a pop song goes viral online, people may use it in videos, dance to it, talk about it with friends, and hear it repeatedly in different places. Before long, the song is no longer just a song; it becomes part of the cultural weather.
components.chinese: 例如，當一首流行歌在網上爆紅時，人們可能會拿它來配影片、跟著跳舞、和朋友討論它，而且在不同地方一再聽見它。沒多久，那首歌就不再只是一首歌，而像是變成了一種文化氣候。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, when a pop song goes viral online, people
may use it in videos, dance to it, talk about it with friends, and hear it
repeatedly in different places. Before long, the song is no longer just
a song; it becomes part of the cultural weather.
[例如，當一首流行歌在網上爆紅時，人們可能會拿它來配影片、跟著跳舞、和朋友討論它，
而且在不同地方一再聽見它。沒多久，那首歌就不再只是一首歌，而像是變成了一種文化氣

components.english: Overall, I would say pop music is so popular because it is catchy, easy to connect with, and constantly present in everyday life. That said, popularity does not always mean greater depth, but pop music certainly knows how to meet people where they are and pull them in.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說流行音樂這麼受歡迎，是因為它上口、容易讓人產生連結，而且在日常生活中無處不在。不過，受歡迎並不一定代表它更有深度；但流行音樂確實很懂得在人們所在的位置和他們相遇，然後把他們吸引進去。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion: Overall, I would say pop music is so popular because
it is catchy, easy to connect with, and constantly present in everyday
life. That said, popularity does not always mean greater depth, but
pop music certainly knows how to meet people where they are and
pull them in.
[總結來說，我會說流行音樂這麼受歡迎，是因為它上口、容易讓人產生連結，而且在日常
生活中無處不在。不過，受歡迎並不一定代表它更有深度；但流行音樂確實很懂得在人們所
在的位置和他們相遇，然後把他們吸引進去。]

question.english: What types of songs are the most popular among today’s young generation in Hong Kong?
question.chinese: 在今天的香港年輕一代之中，哪類歌曲最受歡迎？

source_lines.text: Topic: What types of songs are the most popular among today’s
young generation in Hong Kong?
在今天的香港年輕一代之中，哪類歌曲最受歡迎？

components.english: In Hong Kong, I’d say the most popular songs among young people are mainly Cantopop, K-pop, and English pop, with Mandarin songs also very much part of the mix.
components.chinese: 在香港，我會說年輕人最喜歡的歌曲主要是廣東歌、K-pop 和英文流行歌，而國語歌其實也同樣是整體版圖中很重要的一部分。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: In Hong Kong, I’d say the most popular songs among young
people are mainly Cantopop, K-pop, and English pop, with Mandarin
songs also very much part of the mix.
[在香港，我會說年輕人最喜歡的歌曲主要是廣東歌、K-pop 和英文流行歌，而國語歌其實
也同樣是整體版圖中很重要的一部分。]

components.english: I think that happens because Hong Kong’s music taste has always been quite international. Young people still feel especially close to Cantonese songs, because the language feels familiar and emotionally close to home, but at the same time they are very open to Korean, Mandarin, and English music as well. So their taste is not stuck in one lane.
components.chinese: 我認為之所以會這樣，是因為香港的音樂口味一直都相當國際化。年輕人對廣東歌仍然特別有親近感，因為語言本身就很熟悉，也更容易帶來貼近生活的情感共鳴；但與此同時，他們對韓文、國語和英文音樂也同樣很開放。所以，他們的口味並不是困在單一一條路上。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: I think that happens because Hong Kong’s music taste
has always been quite international. Young people still feel especially
close to Cantonese songs, because the language feels familiar and
emotionally close to home, but at the same time they are very open to
Korean, Mandarin, and English music as well. So their taste is not
stuck in one lane.
[我認為之所以會這樣，是因為香港的音樂口味一直都相當國際化。年輕人對廣東歌仍然特
別有親近感，因為語言本身就很熟悉，也更容易帶來貼近生活的情感共鳴；但與此同時，他
們對韓文、國語和英文音樂也同樣很開放。所以，他們的口味並不是困在單一一條路上。]

components.english: For example, it would not be unusual at all for a young person in Hong Kong to have a Cantonese love song, a K-pop dance track, and an English hit all sitting in the same playlist. That kind of musical mix feels completely normal now.
components.chinese: 例如，一個香港年輕人的播放清單裡，同時放著一首廣東情歌、一首 K-pop 舞曲，以及一首英文熱門歌，這其實一點也不奇怪。這種混合式的聆聽方式，現在已經非常自然。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, it would not be unusual at all for a young
person in Hong Kong to have a Cantonese love song, a K-pop dance
track, and an English hit all sitting in the same playlist. That kind of
musical mix feels completely normal now.
[例如，一個香港年輕人的播放清單裡，同時放著一首廣東情歌、一首 K-pop 舞曲，以及一
首英文熱門歌，這其實一點也不奇怪。這種混合式的聆聽方式，現在已經非常自然。]

components.english: So, if I had to sum it up, I’d say the most popular songs are usually pop songs that are catchy, emotional, and easy to connect with, no matter what language they are in.
components.chinese: 所以，如果要我總結一下，我會說最受歡迎的歌曲通常都是那些旋律抓耳、情感鮮明，而且很容易令人產生共鳴的流行作品，無論它們是用甚麼語言唱的。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, if I had to sum it up, I’d say the most popular songs
are usually pop songs that are catchy, emotional, and easy to connect
with, no matter what language they are in.
[所以，如果要我總結一下，我會說最受歡迎的歌曲通常都是那些旋律抓耳、情感鮮明，而
且很容易令人產生共鳴的流行作品，無論它們是用甚麼語言唱的。]

components.english: That said, I think the real dividing line is often mood rather than genre.
components.chinese: 不過，我覺得真正的分界很多時候其實不是曲風，而是心情。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I think the real dividing line is often mood rather
than genre.
[不過，我覺得真正的分界很多時候其實不是曲風，而是心情。]

components.english: Some young people go for soft, emotional ballads, while others prefer upbeat dance songs or rap when they want more energy. In other words, they do not always choose songs with their heads; quite often, they choose with their mood.
components.chinese: 有些年輕人會偏愛柔和、感性的抒情歌；但也有人在想要更高能量的時候，會選節奏更強的舞曲或 rap。換句話說，他們未必總是用理性去選歌；很多時候，他們其實是在跟著心情選。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: Some young people go for soft, emotional ballads,
while others prefer upbeat dance songs or rap when they want more
energy. In other words, they do not always choose songs with their
heads; quite often, they choose with their mood.
[有些年輕人會偏愛柔和、感性的抒情歌；但也有人在想要更高能量的時候，會選節奏更強
的舞曲或 rap。換句話說，他們未必總是用理性去選歌；很多時候，他們其實是在跟著心情

components.english: For instance, someone might listen to mellow Cantopop at night, switch to K-pop or hip-hop at the gym, and then put on English pop when they just want something light and familiar. So the playlist changes, but the need is the same: they want songs that hit the right note for the moment.
components.chinese: 例如，一個人晚上可能會聽一些比較柔和的廣東歌，在健身的時候就轉去聽 K-pop 或hip-hop，之後如果只是想聽些輕鬆又熟悉的東西，又可能會換成英文流行歌。所以，播放清單會變，但背後的需要其實是一樣的：他們想要的是在那一刻剛好對味的音樂。

source_lines.text: 7. Example 2: For instance, someone might listen to mellow Cantopop
at night, switch to K-pop or hip-hop at the gym, and then put on
English pop when they just want something light and familiar. So the
playlist changes, but the need is the same: they want songs that hit
the right note for the moment.
[例如，一個人晚上可能會聽一些比較柔和的廣東歌，在健身的時候就轉去聽 K-pop 或
hip-hop，之後如果只是想聽些輕鬆又熟悉的東西，又可能會換成英文流行歌。所以，播放清
單會變，但背後的需要其實是一樣的：他們想要的是在那一刻剛好對味的音樂。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say Hong Kong’s young generation still has a strong taste for Cantopop, but their ears are wide open. The most popular songs are the ones that feel current, catchy, and emotionally relatable, rather than songs that feel distant or hard to connect with.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說香港年輕一代對廣東歌仍然有很強的偏愛，但他們的耳朵其實非常開放。最受歡迎的歌曲，通常是那些有當下感、旋律抓耳，而且情感上容易產生共鳴的作品，而不是那些讓人覺得太遙遠或難以投入的歌。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say Hong Kong’s young generation still
has a strong taste for Cantopop, but their ears are wide open. The
most popular songs are the ones that feel current, catchy, and
emotionally relatable, rather than songs that feel distant or hard to
connect with.
[總結來說，我會說香港年輕一代對廣東歌仍然有很強的偏愛，但他們的耳朵其實非常開
放。最受歡迎的歌曲，通常是那些有當下感、旋律抓耳，而且情感上容易產生共鳴的作品，
而不是那些讓人覺得太遙遠或難以投入的歌。]

question.english: How do people get their news in today’s society?
question.chinese: 在今天的社會裡，人們通常怎樣獲取新聞？

source_lines.text: Topic: How do people get their news in today’s society?
在今天的社會裡，人們通常怎樣獲取新聞？

components.english: These days, I’d say most people get their news mainly through their phones, especially through social media, news apps, and online news platforms.
components.chinese: 如今，我會說大多數人主要都是透過手機獲取新聞，特別是透過社交媒體、新聞應用程式，以及網上新聞平台。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: These days, I’d say most people get their news mainly
through their phones, especially through social media, news apps,
and online news platforms.
[如今，我會說大多數人主要都是透過手機獲取新聞，特別是透過社交媒體、新聞應用程式
，以及網上新聞平台。]

components.english: The reason is quite simple: it is fast, convenient, and always within reach. People no longer have to wait for the evening news or buy a newspaper in the morning. News now comes to them almost instantly, often in the middle of the day, while they are commuting, eating lunch, or just scrolling for a few minutes. In a way, news has stopped knocking at the door and started living in people’s pockets.
components.chinese: 原因其實很簡單：這樣既快、又方便，而且隨手可得。人們已經不需要再等晚間新聞，或者一早去買報紙。現在新聞幾乎是即時送到眼前，很多時就在通勤途中、午飯時間，或者只是隨手滑幾分鐘手機的時候看到。某程度上，新聞已經不再是走到門前敲門，而是直接住進了人們的口袋裡。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The reason is quite simple: it is fast, convenient, and
always within reach. People no longer have to wait for the evening
news or buy a newspaper in the morning. News now comes to them
almost instantly, often in the middle of the day, while they are
commuting, eating lunch, or just scrolling for a few minutes. In a way,
news has stopped knocking at the door and started living in people’s
pockets.
[原因其實很簡單：這樣既快、又方便，而且隨手可得。人們已經不需要再等晚間新聞，或者
一早去買報紙。現在新聞幾乎是即時送到眼前，很多時就在通勤途中、午飯時間，或者只是
隨手滑幾分鐘手機的時候看到。某程度上，新聞已經不再是走到門前敲門，而是直接住進了
人們的口袋裡。]

components.english: For example, in Hong Kong, a lot of people wake up and check headlines on their phone before they even get out of bed. Later in the day, they may come across breaking news on Instagram, YouTube, Facebook, or a news app notification without actively looking for it.
components.chinese: 例如，在香港，很多人早上醒來後，甚至還沒下床，就已經先在手機上看新聞標題。到了日間，他們也可能在 Instagram、YouTube、Facebook，或者新聞應用程式的通知裡，不知不覺就接觸到突發消息。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, in Hong Kong, a lot of people wake up and
check headlines on their phone before they even get out of bed. Later
in the day, they may come across breaking news on Instagram,
YouTube, Facebook, or a news app notification without actively
looking for it.
[例如，在香港，很多人早上醒來後，甚至還沒下床，就已經先在手機上看新聞標題。到了日
間，他們也可能在 Instagram、YouTube、Facebook，或者新聞應用程式的通知裡，不知不
覺就接觸到突發消息。]

components.english: So, at the most basic level, I’d say news today reaches people mainly through digital platforms, and especially through the mobile phone.
components.chinese: 所以，最基本來說，我會說，今天的新聞主要是透過數碼平台接觸到人，而當中最核心的工具就是手機。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, at the most basic level, I’d say news today reaches
people mainly through digital platforms, and especially through the
mobile phone.
[所以，最基本來說，我會說，今天的新聞主要是透過數碼平台接觸到人，而當中最核心的
工具就是手機。]

components.english: That said, I do not think people rely on just one source, because many still turn to television, radio, or traditional news organisations when they want something more reliable or more complete.
components.chinese: 不過，我不認為人們只依賴單一來源，因為當他們想要較可靠、較完整的資訊時，很多人仍然會轉向電視、電台，或者較傳統的新聞機構。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I do not think people rely on just one source,
because many still turn to television, radio, or traditional news
organisations when they want something more reliable or more
complete.
[不過，我不認為人們只依賴單一來源，因為當他們想要較可靠、較完整的資訊時，很多人
仍然會轉向電視、電台，或者較傳統的新聞機構。]

components.english: Social media may be quick, but quick does not always mean trustworthy. That is why a lot of people use it as a first alert rather than a final answer. They may see a headline online first, then go to a proper news website, a TV report, or a more established source to check the full story. In other words, news consumption today is often a mix of speed and caution: people want to know fast, but they also want to know right.
components.chinese: 社交媒體也許很快，但快並不一定等於可信。所以，很多人會把它當成第一下提醒，而不是最後答案。他們可能先在網上看到標題，之後再去正式新聞網站、電視報道，或者較有公信力的來源查清楚整件事。換句話說，今天人們接收新聞的方式，很多時候是一種速度和謹慎的混合：大家既想快點知道，也想知道得正確。

always mean trustworthy. That is why a lot of people use it as a first

but they also want to know right.
